title: athyrium dissitifolium var. funebre
intro: Athyrium dissitifolium var. funebre, also known as Japanese lady fern or autumn fern, is a variety of fern with delicate, lacy fronds that are typically found in mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Athyrium dissitifolium var. funebre are thought to include reduced inflammation and improved wound healing, as well as potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried or fresh ingredient in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
